Small samples for easy travel. Not as moisturizing for my skin. I love the small sample size items since I can easily put them in my pocket and take wherever I want. However, it is not a lot for the price you pay for it. I do love Burts bees products but they don't work as well at moisturizing my hands as other products do which is why I took off a star. I have to apply the hand salve and the hand cream religiously to make any kind of difference in my dry hands and it's just not worth the effort of applying it 7+ times a day when I can apply other lotions or creams twice and have a similar effect. The Chapstick are my favorite though, I always have one on me. I have not tried the foot cream yet since I can barely reach my feet being almost 9 months pregnant but I don't have very high hopes with how the other creams work with the skin.

Works, but OILY. Personally, I prefer the bath and body lotions compared to Burt Bees because Burt Bees' lotions and creams seem to be more oily. Additionally, the foot cream isn't cream but more of a paste like toothpaste but more textured, which is off putting. Furthermore, the lotions generally take a longer time to soak in and in that period of time I'm not able to touch my laptop or phone without getting them dirty. Overall, the price is good for the amount of products you get, the scent isn't half bad, and it does hydrate my skin, but personally it is not worth feeling oily. I don't have super dry skin so these products were not for me.

My moms fave. I always buy these for my mom and sisters. It’s a fool proof appreciated practical but luxurious gift. The price is right. Consistent use of good products like these can repair skin. I have to say I gift it because we all love the quality fir the price but we do love the scent. The grape seed honey lotion is a smell that transports. I love it. All the products smell divine but that one is my fave. I have spent the last decade learning a lot about pollinators. Turning my space into a sanctuary for them. The scent of beeswax heating up in a pot or lighting a candle is absolutely aroma therapy at its best. The sun, rain, earth, flowers, and honey may just be the most quintessential compilation of elements for a warm sweet earthy scent that is not cloying. As for the quality of the product it is rejuvenating and great self care. Getting a little collection like this is a mini spa day. And I always get asked why I smell so good! It is a subtle fragrance. If you like that. The other lotion scent I love is Le Occitane lavender. Or Dolce Gabanna The One. If you like those you may enjoy this. If you hate scent or are allergic you may not. The quality is subtle but layered and rich. I enjoy the warm notes. If you like a little fragrance in your lotion that’s not over powering. The scent are all from the earth. Coconut almond honey Grape seed and such.

A FINE collection of products!. My hands get so dry from my job and just from the winter time being brutally dry where I live. I wanted to try something besides regular lotion to help my hands. They were covered in dry patches that itched, and the skin around my nails was peeling and bleeding constantly. I didn’t know exactly what I wanted, but I like Burt’s a lot, and this set came with a few different products to try before committing to a full size of any one product. I love everything in the set. The lip balm was a favorite of mine before receiving this, and I’d highly recommend it to anyone. The salve is a blessing. The honey hand cream is great to keep in my purse and use whenever. The almond & milk hand creme smells incredible. The cuticle butter is a nice addition. I use the hand creams and salve daily, and the cuticle cream every few days when I clean and trim my nails. I use the foot cream after every shower, and I’m not a huge fan of the coconut smell, but it has completely rejuvenated my dry cracking feet.

I don't typically buy Burt's Bees products so I was curious when I saw this kit and decided to give it a whirl when I was in need of a foot cream, but am sad to say that I was kind of overall disappointed. Everything in here just felt plain weird on my skin. It was like a slightly greasy film more than anything. The foot cream is not really a cream at all, but rather a thick gel-like consistency that resembles Neosporin and spreads about the same, which I guess isn't awful and didn't really bother my feet because I couldn't really feel it on them, but isn't what I expected and definitely had to wear socks over it because the feeling against my sheets was atrocious. The almond and milk hand cream is really cute and smells nice, but feels greasy too and doesn't absorb all too well like everything else in here, and I had higher hopes for it than the hand lotion. I can't really complain about the rest of the kit though. A salve seems like it's supposed to sort of feel that way and I typically use something like an oil for my cuticles anyways. Everything here also has a very distinct fragrance to them, so anyone sensitive to that take caution.

So I am satisfied with this set. I got this set, for snowy climate on vacation. The hand lotion feels a little sticky, and the foot lotion is very thick and gel-like. Nevertheless, all of the products kept my skin from cracking and drying out, and that's the main goal! So I am satisfied with this set. I'm still using the Pom chapstick, in hot weather! It adds some color, and it does help lips from drying out. I enjoy using it. *Interesting tip: If you usually suffer from chapped nose during severe colds, use the B.B. plain chapstick (no added color) around your nostrils after blowing your nose. It will keep them from cracking and blistering. This has been very helpful for me in the past.

Honestly, this is the first lip balm I used completely- without loosing, washing, or having my dog eat (hounds are stupid) I Love the lip balm, the honey hand creme was soooooo lovely, made me feel silky and smelled awesome. The cuticle cream I kept in my purse and would apply daily, I love the lemon smell, so fresh, and it made my cuticles look and feel softer. The coconut foot creme, I liked that, but maybe the scent wasn't quite to my liking, didn't seem very coconutty to me, but that's merely my opinion. It did, however, leave my feet feeling soft and smooth, I like to put it on at night time in bed before I went to sleep, I think also the foot creme should have been slightly bigger then the hand lotion bottle, only because I think me and maybe most other people (correct me if I'm wrong) might use more per use on our feet vs or hands. I found I used up the foot stuff way faster, anyway. But I loved it and will definitely be getting more lip balms and cuticle creme for sure

Great products, good price for a sampler.. This is a really great sampler, and the price I paid was fantastic. Burt's Bees stuff is not super cheap, so this is a great way to try out several of their best items for not much money. I was thinking of getting a full size tin of the lemon cuticle butter, since I have one at work I use often because it's amazing. Instead I got this whole set for just a few dollars more. These are sampler sizes, so they're small, but that's great for carrying in a purse or pocket. The cuticle butter (which smells delicious!) will last ages even in this small tin, and the lip balm is full size. I put on the honey hand cream first thing, and it smells wonderful and feels great. Not sticky at all, just softening. I know from past experience I like the foot cream, and am looking forward to trying out the almond & milk hand cream. The hand salve is fantastic - my dad who is a mechanic and has the hands to prove it recommends it highly. It smells like rosemary. I'm very pleased with this set, and looking forward to using it all up... and I might buy this same set again when it's all gone.

Great way to try products, but don't share.. I bought this for myself last year right before about two months of frequent air travel, and I was SO glad I did. The items in this kit saved my skin, took up almost no room in my bags, and gave me a way to try new products that I ordinarily wouldn't splurge on. The two hand balms last forever; so does the lip balm. The hand cream was the only thing I couldn't try due to a really annoying allergy that I have, but my mom was happy to have it and said she liked it. The tubes of hand and foot lotion are tricky because they get used up FAST if you dare to share (translation: DON'T SHARE!). Tip: make sure to close the tubes completely, especially the foot moisturizer. If you're not careful or leave it without a cap, it WILL leak. This set has made a lovely gift a few times since I tried it in my family. Perfect for that one super-busy family member/friend/student/teacher we all know.

Sticky, sticky, sticky. I was excited to receive this as a gift because I wanted to try the products. Context: I'm dark skinned, I like the outdoors, and I cook. Almond Milk Beeswax Hand Cream: I washed my hands after lunch, then tried this. Beeswax is right - very waxy and gluey, and took forever to absorb. Smells like almond paste. I would NOT keep this in my desk at work. Pomegranate Lip Balm: The hint of color is invisible on my lips. No SPF, so not suitable for daytime wear for me. Honey-Grapeseed Hand Cream: Sticky. Takes forever to absorb. It's honey, I get it, but I need to be able to moisturize my hands and move on without the things I touch getting sticky. Would definitely not keep at work or in the car. Coconut Foot Cream: Super goopy and sticky. I put it on my feet one morning (and had to wait forever for it to absorb into my hands even after I washed my hands), then put on wool socks and boots and went about my day. When I showered at night, the tub floor became VERY slippery. Lemon Butter Cuticle Cream: I like the smell of lemon sugar and this was the only part of the gift set I genuinely liked and would use, so I searched Target for the cuticle cream by itself. I thought I'd use it as a hand lotion before bed. Turns out it's about ten dollars. Ehh... I'll stick to my $6 unscented body lotion and $2 cocoa butter lip balm with SPF.

Great Size, Very Good For Purse. First of all, if you think that these are full sized, please read the description carefully. I got these a stocking stuffer this past Christmas, and was really pleased! I am a die-hard Burt's Bees fan, and when I got these little testers, I was ecstatic. They measure a couple inches talk, and the salves are about an inch in diameter. They all smell very good, and go on pretty smoothly. Lotion: The smell is to DIE for. It is very long wearing, and doesn't absorb right away. It keeps skin moisturized for a long period of time. I use it at night before bed, or keep it in my purse while I am at work. It's been really good for this winter, and healed cracking in between my fingers! Foot Cream: It has a very thick texture, and is best used before bed, or after a shower. I rub it into my feet and let the product soak in a little bit. Afterwards, I put on cotton socks and let it soak in over night. I wake up with lovely, soft feet. Salve: It is very thick, and very moisturizing. I also keep this in my my purse and use it for any cracking in between my fingers as well as my cuticles. It stays in place, and doesn't absorb too quickly. (I like it, others may not) Altogether, I really love the products. I bought a full size of the lotion, and have fallen madly in love. I'm sad to see my mini-lotion run out in the near future, so investing in the full size is worth it!
